ontology.foodon_gap_note: FoodOn has a biryani entry (FOODON_00002809) but no specific prawn biryani combined set-meal entry. Used as the closest defensible anchor for the biryani component; chilli chicken and cola components are not individually coded here.
ontology.foodex2_description: Composite dish — mixed rice-based meal with meat and beverage
ontology.foodex2_gap_note: FoodEx2 does not have a specific code for a prawn biryani + chilli chicken + cola set meal. A0CKP (composite dish) is used as the closest defensible FoodEx2 anchor. Individual component codes: rice dish (A0CJX), poultry dish (A0CKN), carbonated soft drink (A0EKG).
ontology.snomed_ct_gap_note: No SNOMED CT code exists for this composite Indian set meal. SNOMED CT does not catalogue restaurant meal sets at this level of specificity.

serving.description: 1 set meal (Prawn Biryani + Chilli Chicken + Coke)
serving.natural_language_portion: One standard set meal comprising approximately 400 g prawn biryani, 350 g chilli chicken, and 250 g (approximately 250 ml) Coca-Cola, totalling roughly 1,000 g as listed on the GrabFood platform.

nutrient_source.title: ICMR-NIN Nutritive Value of Indian Foods (2017) and USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy — composite estimation
nutrient_source.organization: National Institute of Nutrition (ICMR-NIN), India; U.S. Department of Agriculture
nutrient_source.source_match_notes: No single approved source contains a composite prawn biryani + chilli chicken + cola set meal. Nutrient values are estimated by weighted-average composition: approximately 40% prawn biryani (ICMR-NIN prawn biryani reference), 35% chilli chicken (USDA FoodData Central chicken curry / stir-fried chicken with sauce analogue), and 25% Coca-Cola (USDA FoodData Central, Carbonated beverage, cola, SR Legacy item 14400). The input per_100g values (energy 150 kcal, protein 8 g, fat 7.5 g, carbohydrate 13 g) are broadly consistent with this weighted estimate and are retained with medium confidence. Sodium 280 mg/100 g is plausible given soy sauce in chilli chicken and spiced rice. All values are flagged as estimated.

gi_source.title: International tables of glycemic index and glycemic load values: 2021 — Atkinson et al.; University of Sydney GI Database — basmati rice and cola references
gi_source.organization: University of Sydney GI Database; American Journal of Clinical Nutrition
gi_source.source_match_notes: No GI value exists for this exact composite set meal. GI estimated as weighted average: basmati rice GI ≈ 58 (University of Sydney GI Database, tested value), chilli chicken (protein-fat dominant, minimal carbohydrate contribution, GI effect negligible), Coca-Cola GI ≈ 63 (University of Sydney GI Database). Weighted composite GI ≈ 60 (medium). Glycemic load per serving calculated as: (GI × carbohydrate_g per serving) / 100 = (60 × 130 g carbohydrate in 1,000 g serving) / 100 = 78.0. Carbohydrate per serving = 13 g/100 g × 1,000 g = 130 g.

hypertension.key_factor: Sugar-sweetened beverage (Coca-Cola) and elevated sodium from soy sauce in chilli chicken (280 mg sodium per 100 g; ~2,800 mg per full set meal)
hypertension.guidance: Individuals with hypertension should eliminate the Coca-Cola component entirely and request reduced-sodium soy sauce in the chilli chicken, as the combined sodium load of approximately 2,800 mg per set meal substantially exceeds the WHO recommended daily limit of 2,000 mg.

type2_diabetes.key_factor: Sugar-sweetened beverage (Coca-Cola) and high glycemic load (GL ≈ 78 per set meal) from basmati rice combined with low dietary fibre (0.8 g per 100 g)
type2_diabetes.guidance: Individuals with Type 2 Diabetes should avoid this set meal as consumed; the Coca-Cola alone provides rapidly absorbed free sugars that cause acute postprandial glucose spikes, and the overall glycemic load of approximately 78 per serving far exceeds the recommended per-meal GL target of 10–20 for glycaemic management.

cardiovascular_disease.key_factor: Saturated fat from ghee and deep-fried chicken (2.5 g saturated fat per 100 g; ~25 g per set meal), trans fat (0.05 g per 100 g), high sodium (~2,800 mg per set meal), and free sugars from Coca-Cola
cardiovascular_disease.guidance: Individuals with cardiovascular disease should avoid this set meal due to the combination of high saturated fat from ghee and deep-frying, elevated sodium exceeding daily limits, and free sugars from Coca-Cola, all of which independently and synergistically elevate cardiovascular risk; substituting Coke with water, requesting grilled rather than fried chicken, and reducing ghee would substantially lower the risk profile.

rheumatoid_arthritis.key_factor: Deep-fried preparation generating advanced glycation end-products (AGEs); nightshade vegetables (bell pepper, green chilli, tomato) in chilli chicken
rheumatoid_arthritis.guidance: Individuals with rheumatoid arthritis should request chilli chicken grilled or pan-fried rather than deep-fried to reduce AGE formation, and should be aware that bell peppers, green chillies, and tomatoes are nightshade vegetables that may exacerbate joint inflammation in nightshade-sensitive individuals.

llm_training.natural_language_description: The Prawn Biryani, Chilli Chicken and Coke Set Meal is a popular Indian restaurant set meal sold on food-delivery platforms in Singapore. It comprises three components: (1) Prawn Biryani — long-grain basmati rice layered with marinated prawns, whole spices (cardamom, cloves, bay leaf), saffron or turmeric, caramelised onions, and ghee, slow-cooked using the dum (sealed-pot steam) method to produce aromatic, fluffy rice with tender, spiced prawns; (2) Chilli Chicken — boneless chicken pieces marinated in soy sauce, ginger, garlic, and chilli, then deep-fried or pan-fried and tossed in a spicy, tangy Indo-Chinese sauce containing bell peppers, green chillies, tomato, and soy sauce; and (3) Coca-Cola — a standard 250 ml serving of the carbonated cola beverage. The set meal weighs approximately 1,000 g in total. Per 100 g, the composite provides approximately 150 kcal energy, 8 g protein, 7.5 g fat (2.5 g saturated), 13 g carbohydrate, 4.5 g sugar, 0.8 g dietary fibre, and 280 mg sodium. The glycemic index of the composite is estimated at approximately 60 (medium GI), driven primarily by basmati rice (GI ≈ 58) and Coca-Cola (GI ≈ 63), with a high glycemic load of approximately 78 per full set meal. Clinically, this meal is unsuitable for individuals with Type 2 Diabetes due to the sugar-sweetened beverage and high glycemic load; unsuitable for hypertension due to sodium exceeding 2,800 mg per serving and the SSB component; and unsuitable for cardiovascular disease due to saturated fat, trans fat, high sodium, and free sugars. The meal is halal-certified in most Singapore Indian restaurant contexts. It is a filling, flavour-rich meal combining seafood, poultry, and a carbonated beverage, representative of the Indo-Chinese fusion cuisine popular in Singapore's Indian food scene.

confidence_reason: No single approved source contains this exact composite set meal. Nutrient values are estimated by weighted-average composition from ICMR-NIN (prawn biryani component) and USDA FoodData Central (chilli chicken analogue and Coca-Cola). The input per_100g values are broadly consistent with this estimation and are retained. GI is an analogous estimate from University of Sydney GI Database references for basmati rice and cola. Confidence is medium due to the composite nature of the dish and the absence of a direct tested record.
notes: 1. COMPOSITE MEAL ESTIMATION: This record represents a three-component set meal (prawn biryani + chilli chicken + Coca-Cola). No single approved source provides a composite nutrient profile for this exact combination. Values are estimated from ICMR-NIN (prawn biryani) and USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy (chilli chicken analogue; Coca-Cola item 14400) using approximate weight proportions (40% biryani, 35% chilli chicken, 25% cola). 2. NEGATIVE SENTINEL VALUES (-1): monounsaturated_fat_g, polyunsaturated_fat_g, potassium_mg, vitamin_a_mcg_rae, vitamin_b1_thiamine_mg, vitamin_b2_riboflavin_mg, vitamin_b3_niacin_mg, vitamin_b6_mg, vitamin_b12_mcg, folate_mcg_dfe, vitamin_d_mcg, vitamin_e_mg, phosphorus_mg, magnesium_mg, zinc_mg, selenium_mcg, and water_g are set to -1 because detailed fatty acid fractions, micronutrient profiles, and moisture data for this composite Indian set meal are not available in ICMR-NIN or USDA FoodData Central at the composite level as of April 2026. 3. TRANS FAT: trans_fat_g is set to 0.05 g/100 g (trace level) consistent with ghee and deep-frying contributions; this is a plausible estimate, not a confirmed tested value. 4. GI ESTIMATION: GI of 60 is a weighted analogous estimate based on basmati rice (GI ≈ 58, University of Sydney GI Database) and Coca-Cola (GI ≈ 63, University of Sydney GI Database). No direct GI test exists for this composite meal. 5. ETHNIC TAGS: The input listed 'Butter Chicken' and 'Curry' as ethnic tags; these are not applicable to this specific dish and have been removed. The dish is Indian (Indo-Chinese fusion for chilli chicken component). 6. ONTOLOGY: FoodOn FOODON_00002809 (biryani) used as approximate anchor. FoodEx2 A0CKP (composite dish) used as approximate anchor. No SNOMED CT code applicable. 7. ALLERGENS: Prawns (crustacean shellfish) and soy sauce (gluten/wheat) are primary allergens. 8. HALAL STATUS: Marked halal based on input dietary_tags; verification with specific restaurant is recommended.
